theme_and_archetype.description: Designed for tradies and supervisors on active construction sites. The UI avoids soft, playful SaaS curves. It relies on high-visibility 'Safety Orange', hard borders, and maximum contrast. Generous hit areas and blocky layouts ensure easy operation with gloved hands in bright sunlight.

typography.font_strategy: High-legibility geometric sans-serif for body, condensed/heavy weights for headings. Use system default sans-serif as the base, keeping weights bold and distinct.

touch_targets.description: CRITICAL: All buttons, inputs, and list items must be at least 56px tall to accommodate thick gloves.

border_radius.description: Keep components boxy and rigid. Max radius is 8px. Do not use pill-shaped buttons.

login.layout: Center-aligned content with a heavy industrial logo header. Full-width primary button. High contrast background.

dashboard.layout: Bento-style grid for metrics (Current Status, Overdue). Uses cards with thick colored left-borders (Green/Yellow/Red) to indicate status severity at a glance. Quick action buttons for Check-out/Check-in fixed at the bottom.

asset_register.layout: Search bar at top (sticky). Horizontal scrolling pill-list for categories. Flat list of assets. Each list item should be minimum 80px height, containing a square image thumbnail, bold title, and status badge.

asset_detail.layout: Large hero image (max 40% screen height). Tactical specs layout (2-column grid for Brand, Model, Serial). Large full-width Check In / Check Out button pinned to safe area bottom.

forms_check_in_out.layout: Step-by-step or single long scrolling form with massive touch targets.
forms_check_in_out.check_in_specifics: Condition selector MUST be 3 massive color-coded buttons side-by-side (Green: Good, Yellow: Fair, Red: Damaged). If Damaged is selected, a 'Take Photo' button appears.
forms_check_in_out.qr_scanner: Include a prominent 'Scan QR' button with a camera icon near the asset selection field. Even if fully functional later, the UI must have the entry point ready.

gardening.alt: gardening gloves, saw, and pruning shears

testing.data_testid_rules: All interactive elements (buttons, inputs, tabs, list items) MUST include a data-testid attribute. Use kebab-case and role-based naming (e.g., 'login-submit-button', 'asset-item-123', 'scan-qr-button').

instructions_to_main_agent: Initialize standard Expo project with React Navigation.
Ensure all touchable areas (Pressable, TouchableOpacity) wrap their content completely and enforce the 56px minimum height.
Avoid generic border radii. The UI should look 'blocky' (max 4px-8px radius).
Use safe area insets to prevent UI from hiding behind notches, but pin primary action buttons (like Submit or Scan QR) strictly to the bottom of the screen.
For the MVP, dummy data for the ~10 assets should use the Unsplash/Pexels URLs provided in this file.
Build the core JWT structure with a mock backend or simple Context API for now, simulating Admin, Supervisor, and Trade roles.
